title: Quiz 8
subtitle: B1-B2 Explorer Challenge
source: Original B1-B2 quiz created for this system

questions.question: This time next week, I _____ to Wellington.
questions.explanation: Future continuous uses will be + verb-ing. It describes an action in progress at a specific future time.

questions.question: While I _____ for my keys, I _____ an old photo under the sofa.
questions.explanation: Use past continuous for the longer background action and past simple for the shorter completed action.

questions.skill: Would for Future in the Past
questions.question: Dad said he _____ us up at six, but he arrived at half past.
questions.explanation: Would can describe a future action from a past point of view: Dad said he would pick us up.

questions.question: Lena is used to _____ in a noisy classroom.
questions.explanation: Be used to means something feels normal. It is followed by a noun or verb-ing.

questions.question: It took me months to get used to _____ on the left side of the road.
questions.explanation: Get used to means slowly become comfortable with something. It is followed by verb-ing.

questions.question: You _____ check your answers before you hand in the test.
questions.explanation: Had better gives strong advice. It is followed by the base verb.

questions.question: I would rather _____ at home tonight than go to a noisy party.
questions.explanation: Would rather is followed by the base verb when the subject is the same.

questions.question: I prefer reading paper books to _____ long stories on a screen.
questions.explanation: Prefer + verb-ing to + verb-ing compares two activities.

questions.question: It was _____ difficult puzzle that nobody solved it before lunch.
questions.explanation: Use such a/an + adjective + singular countable noun: such a difficult puzzle.

questions.question: The box was too heavy for me _____ by myself.
questions.explanation: The pattern is too + adjective + for someone + to + base verb.

questions.question: Safety glasses must _____ in the science lab.
questions.explanation: Passive with a modal uses modal + be + past participle: must be worn.

questions.question: The librarian asked us _____ quietly near the reading room.
questions.explanation: Reported requests often use ask + object + to + base verb.

questions.question: Oscar _____ me that he had already finished the poster.
questions.explanation: Tell is usually followed by an object: told me. Say does not take the object in this pattern.

questions.question: Please remember _____ the file before you close the laptop.
questions.explanation: Remember to do something means do not forget to do it.

questions.question: We stopped _____ a map because we were completely lost.
questions.explanation: Stop to do something means pause one action in order to do another.

questions.question: You are responsible _____ feeding the fish while I am away.
questions.explanation: The adjective responsible is followed by for.

questions.question: The class moved so fast that I could hardly _____ the notes.
questions.explanation: Keep up with means move or learn at the same speed as someone or something.

questions.question: The coach promised to _____ the problem with the broken scoreboard.
questions.explanation: Look into means investigate or examine a problem.

questions.question: The new student turned out _____ a brilliant chess player.
questions.explanation: Turn out to be means finally be discovered to be something.

questions.question: The final _____ is yours, so think carefully. (decide)
questions.explanation: Decide is a verb. Decision is the noun.

questions.question: A _____ solution is often better than an obvious one. (create)
questions.explanation: Create is a verb. Creative is the adjective.

questions.question: You need _____ before you can use the school camera. (permit)
questions.explanation: Permit can be a verb. Permission is the noun meaning being allowed to do something.

questions.question: Put the words in the correct order:
questions.correctSentence: At eight o'clock tomorrow I will be travelling to Wellington
questions.explanation: Future continuous shows an action in progress at a future time: will be travelling.

questions.question: Put the words in the correct order:
questions.correctSentence: It took me two hours to finish the essay
questions.explanation: The pattern is It took + person + time + to + verb.

questions.reading: Jules joined the school garden club because he wanted to spend more time outside. At first, he thought the club's careful record keeping was boring. However, when the tomatoes did not grow well, the notes showed that the plants had been watered too often. The club changed its plan, and by the end of term the students sold enough vegetables to buy new books for the library. Jules realized that science could be practical, not just something in a textbook.
questions.question: Why did Jules first join the garden club?
questions.explanation: The text says he joined because he wanted to spend more time outside.
options: He wanted to spend time outside

questions.reading: Jules joined the school garden club because he wanted to spend more time outside. At first, he thought the club's careful record keeping was boring. However, when the tomatoes did not grow well, the notes showed that the plants had been watered too often. The club changed its plan, and by the end of term the students sold enough vegetables to buy new books for the library. Jules realized that science could be practical, not just something in a textbook.
questions.question: What did the notes show?
questions.explanation: The notes showed that the tomatoes had been watered too often.
options: The plants had been watered too often

questions.reading: Jules joined the school garden club because he wanted to spend more time outside. At first, he thought the club's careful record keeping was boring. However, when the tomatoes did not grow well, the notes showed that the plants had been watered too often. The club changed its plan, and by the end of term the students sold enough vegetables to buy new books for the library. Jules realized that science could be practical, not just something in a textbook.
questions.question: What did Jules learn?
questions.explanation: He learned that science can help solve real problems, so it can be practical.

questions.question: You _____ me you were coming; I would have cooked more food.
questions.explanation: Should have + past participle can show criticism or regret about a past action.

questions.question: No sooner _____ the door than the alarm started ringing.
questions.explanation: After no sooner at the beginning, use inversion: No sooner had I opened... than...

questions.question: Little _____ that the small mistake would become a big problem.
questions.explanation: Little did I know is a fixed inverted structure meaning I did not know at all.

questions.question: The camera will not work _____ you charge the battery.
questions.explanation: Unless means if not. The camera will not work if you do not charge the battery.

questions.question: You can join the trip _____ you bring a signed permission form.
questions.explanation: As long as means only if. It introduces a condition.

questions.question: _____ it rains, the match will continue because the field has a roof.
questions.explanation: Even if means something may happen, but it will not change the result.

questions.question: _____ fire, use the stairs and leave the building calmly.
questions.explanation: In case of + noun is common in formal instructions and emergency notices.

questions.question: I'd rather you _____ anyone about my surprise party.
questions.explanation: Would rather + another subject uses a past form for a present or future preference.

questions.question: The experiment was _____ because everyone followed the instructions. (success)
questions.explanation: Success is a noun. Successful is the adjective.

questions.question: Ben's _____ made him answer before he had read the whole question. (impatient)
questions.explanation: Impatient is an adjective. Impatience is the noun.

questions.reading: Sofia found a notebook on the bus. It was full of sketches and story ideas, but there was no name inside. The only clue was a receipt from the town library. Instead of keeping the notebook, Sofia took it to the librarian, who recognized the drawing style of a quiet student named Ethan. When Ethan got the notebook back, he was embarrassed but grateful. A week later, he and Sofia started a lunchtime comics club.
questions.question: What clue helped Sofia return the notebook?
questions.explanation: The notebook had a receipt from the town library.

questions.reading: Sofia found a notebook on the bus. It was full of sketches and story ideas, but there was no name inside. The only clue was a receipt from the town library. Instead of keeping the notebook, Sofia took it to the librarian, who recognized the drawing style of a quiet student named Ethan. When Ethan got the notebook back, he was embarrassed but grateful. A week later, he and Sofia started a lunchtime comics club.
questions.question: Why did Sofia take the notebook to the librarian?
questions.explanation: She wanted help finding the owner, and the library receipt was the best clue.
options: She wanted help finding the owner

questions.reading: Sofia found a notebook on the bus. It was full of sketches and story ideas, but there was no name inside. The only clue was a receipt from the town library. Instead of keeping the notebook, Sofia took it to the librarian, who recognized the drawing style of a quiet student named Ethan. When Ethan got the notebook back, he was embarrassed but grateful. A week later, he and Sofia started a lunchtime comics club.
questions.question: What can we infer about Sofia?
questions.explanation: She returned something valuable to its owner, so she was honest and thoughtful.
options: She knew Ethan very well already

questions.question: Which sentence is correct?
questions.explanation: After suggest that, careful English can use the base verb: he try.
options: I suggested that he tries the easier route.
I suggested that he try the easier route.
I suggested him to try the easier route.
I suggested he trying the easier route.

questions.question: _____ being nervous, Maya gave a clear speech.
questions.explanation: Despite is followed by a noun or verb-ing, not a full clause with subject + verb.

questions.question: There was hardly _____ time left, so we had to hurry.
questions.explanation: Hardly any means almost no. It can be used with uncountable nouns like time.

questions.question: Not until the final page _____ the answer.
questions.explanation: After not until at the beginning, use inversion: did I understand.

questions.question: During her _____, the team had to choose a new leader. (absent)
questions.explanation: Absent is an adjective. Absence is the noun.

questions.question: The club discussed _____ problems such as plastic waste. (environment)
questions.explanation: Environment is a noun. Environmental is the adjective.

questions.question: The class has _____ good progress since the start of term.
questions.explanation: The natural collocation is make progress. In present perfect: has made progress.

questions.question: Put the words in the correct order:
questions.correctSentence: Not only did she finish the race but she also smiled
questions.explanation: When not only starts the sentence, use inversion: did she finish.

questions.question: Put the words in the correct order:
questions.correctSentence: I am slowly getting used to waking up early
questions.explanation: Get used to is followed by verb-ing: getting used to waking up.

questions.question: Which sentence sounds most natural?
questions.explanation: As good as is used for comparison. The full sentence is: The film wasn't as good as I expected.
options: The film wasn't so good than I expected.
The film wasn't as good as I expected.
The film wasn't good as I expected.
The film wasn't as better as I expected.
